title: Metformin Side Effects in Women: What You Need to Know
content:
Introduction

Metformin is commonly prescribed to manage type 2 diabetes and is also used for polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS). While it is generally safe, some women may experience side effects.

Common Side Effects

Digestive Problems

Nausea, diarrhea, and stomach discomfort are common when starting Metformin.

Vitamin B12 Deficiency

Long-term use of Metformin may reduce vitamin B12 absorption which can lead to fatigue or nerve symptoms.

Hormonal Changes

In women with PCOS, Metformin can help regulate menstrual cycles and improve ovulation.

Less Common Effects

  • Metallic taste
  • Mild weight loss
  • Skin rash

Rare But Serious Risk

Lactic acidosis is extremely rare but serious. Symptoms include breathing difficulty, severe weakness, and muscle pain.

Conclusion

Metformin remains one of the most effective medications for managing diabetes and PCOS. Most side effects are mild and improve over time.
